response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist senior engineer with broad full-stack, DLang, and low-latency systems experience, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements: his professional C++ exposure is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ required. He has no Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience (RIDE Capital was retail investment tooling in Node/TypeScript, not investment banking), and his SQL background lists MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle. The gap between his profile and this specialized role is too large to justify an interview.
strengths: Strong systems-level engineering background, including low-latency, high-throughput services (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Solid software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing tooling authorship
Broad polyglot experience (DLang, TypeScript, Java, C#) with demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience (2014–2015), far below the mandatory 5–10 years
No Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No stated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain
